name: State Bank & Trust
description_short: Locally-owned bank serving Winfield, Alabama and surrounding areas for over 100 years with checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and online banking.
description_long:
State Bank & Trust was founded in 1907 as Winfield State Bank by P.M.R. Spann, William M. Ward, Gayle Ford, and James Garrett Stalcup. The bank remained under Stalcup family control until 1978, when it was purchased by Danny McDaniel, Don Bussey, and Joe Bussey. Today, it is still locally owned and operated, with current leadership including the daughter and grandson of Danny McDaniel. The institution has grown from approximately $10 million in assets at the time of purchase to around $300 million today, making it a significant regional financial institution.

State Bank & Trust offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including everyday checking and savings accounts, online banking with instant access to funds, debit cards with SecureLOCK Equip App control, personal loans for various needs, and mortgage products with competitive rates. The bank emphasizes convenience and accessibility, promoting their online banking platform as available anytime and anywhere. They position themselves as a full-service community bank capable of meeting the financial needs of families and businesses.

What distinguishes State Bank & Trust is its deep local roots and community commitment. The bank remained open during the 1930 financial crisis and Great Depression when many institutions failed. Their community involvement is substantial, including support for local schools through scholarships and sponsorships, sponsorship of Relay for Life, and volunteer participation in Habitat for Humanity, the Northwest Alabama Kidney Foundation, and the Joseph F. Weldy Home for Children. The bank explicitly frames banking as fundamentally about people and relationships, with staff providing personalized service to customers.

State Bank & Trust is a legitimate regional community bank with stable ownership, significant assets, and documented community engagement. However, as a local institution, they likely have limited service areas, no national presence, and potentially higher fees compared to large national banks. Customers should verify that their specific financial needs and location fall within the bank's service territory before opening an account. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit cards with SecureLOCK Equip App control
Community financial services and customer support
pros: Over 100 years of continuous operation and community service since 1907
Locally owned and operated with stable, family-connected leadership
Grew from $10 million to $300 million in assets, demonstrating financial strength and growth
Remained solvent during the 1930 Great Depression when many banks failed
Comprehensive online banking available anytime and anywhere
SecureLOCK Equip App for debit card transaction control and security
Extensive community involvement including scholarships, sponsorships, and volunteer programs
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Winfield, Alabama and surrounding areas only
No mention of competitive rates or fee structures compared to regional or national banks
No information provided about minimum balance requirements, monthly fees, or account terms
Limited digital innovation compared to modern neobanks and fintech alternatives
No mention of FDIC insurance details, investment products, or wealth management services
best_for: Long-term Winfield, Alabama residents seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-makers
Small business owners and families in the area needing comprehensive lending and mortgage solutions
Community-minded customers who value local bank support of schools and civic organizations
Customers prioritizing stability and local ownership over cutting-edge digital-first banking
diagnosis: State Bank & Trust is best for established residents of Winfield, Alabama and surrounding areas seeking a community-oriented bank with deep local roots, personalized service, and commitment to regional development. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank serves a specific local market and is not accessible to customers outside the Winfield area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
